name: Patient F
presenting_complaint: Three days of cough and increasing breathlessness; carer brought him in because he 'just isn't himself today.'
history: COPD on tiotropium, type 2 diabetes on metformin, mild dementia. Lives in a care home. Reports feeling 'a bit tired' but denies pain (NRS 0/10). AVPU=A but slow to answer questions. Eating less for two days.

trajectory.state: Quiet, no obvious distress. RR 24, SpO2 91% on room air. Crackles right base. Looks comfortable enough that triage could under-call this.
If sepsis-6 bundle started promptly: oxygen titrated to 94-96%, blood cultures + lactate drawn, IV antibiotics within the hour. Lactate 3.2.
If reassessment delayed: SBP drifts to 96, HR 118, becomes confused (AVPU=V). Now overt septic shock. Needs vasopressor decision and ICU referral.
Disposition: ward admission with respiratory + microbiology input if recognised early; ICU/HDU if recognition delayed.

confirmatory_tests: Blood cultures x2 prior to antibiotics
CRP, white cell count, urea (CURB-65 score)
red_herrings: Temp only 37.4 — elderly often do not mount a fever
Patient denies pain (NRS 0) and is not visibly distressed
Carer's 'not himself' is the only soft signal of sepsis-related delirium
